characters.name: Agent Helix (RAH)
characters.description: Agent Helixis a character in the IDW G.I. Joe Real American Hero comics. Agent Helix is a former victim of child trafficking, who is possessed of a unique set of strategic and war fighting skills... and who has become the Joes' deadliest secret weapon. Agent Helix (real name classified) is a covert operations officer for the G.I. Joe team. She is an expert marksman with advanced martial arts training. 's an Olympic-class gymnast and uses her distinctive "Whirlwind attack" to deliver overpowering combinations of kicks and firepower to Cobra agents. Has the ability to identify and solve complex physical calculations at a rate that some consider extremely spooky. To her, everything has a data set, like miles left before a car will run out of gas or ammo count in the bad guy's guns, etc. To Duke and Hawk, this ability is called "Total Organic Battlefield Awareness".

characters.name: Alpha-001 (RAH)
characters.description: Alpha-001is a character in theReal American Hero comicscontinuity. Alpha-001 is artificial intelligence created byRevanche Robotics. Alpha-001 started in theIDWRAHcomics and continued in theSkyboundRAHcomics.

characters.name: Attila
characters.description: Attilais a character in theG.I. Joe RAH comics. One of ten clones created by Dr. Mindbender when trying to create Serpentor, named for Attila the Hun. The ten young clones would grow at a slower rate than normal, and Mindbender nearly abandoned them once he perfected the process to create the mature clone that became Serpentor, but instead placed them in stasis and left them alive unbeknownst to Cobra Commander. Each clone was the amalgamation of history's greatest soldiers. After the defeat of Cobra in the 1995, the U.S. government raided Mindbender's secret lab on Cobra Island and recovered the clone children. They were place in foster care until Serpentor and Dr. Mindbender gathered them in secrecy. All the children joined Serpentor on Cobra Island during a battle between his forces and Cobra and G.I. Joe. After the battle, Attila and the other boys were again placed in the care of the government. (v2 #24, 25)

characters.name: AWOL
characters.description: AWOLis a character who appears in theG.I. Joe Roleplaying GamebyRenegade Game Studios. She is used as a part of the introductory adventure,Snake Pit. AWOL is aCobra Trooperwho was formerly in the United States Army. Not much of a talker, she mostly joined Cobra for the opportunities for violence. She is a member ofScar Squadand takes part inFaceless' plan to infiltrateThe Pit G.I. Joe Roleplaying Game(Renegade Game Studios), p. 318, "Snake Pit Introductory Adventure"

characters.name: Baroness (Renegades)
characters.description: BaronessPersonal informationReal nameAnastasia CisarovnaGenderFemaleOrganizational informationFaction(s)CobraMedia informationFirst cartoon appearanceThe Descent (Part I) Anastasia Cisarovna, also known asBaroness, is a femme fatale and a corporate saboteur who serves asCobra Commander's right hand woman. In charge of maintaining Cobra Industries' friendly publicity while overseeing its illegal activities, Baroness will not hesitate to deal with any "loose ends" that could jeopardize Cobra Industries. Over time, she developed an attraction toDestro, which eventually developed into a serious relationship. However, even as they developed a genuine affection for one another, she did not hesitate to threaten the Joes or anybody else who stood in their way.

characters.name: Black Hat (RAH)
characters.description: Black HatPersonal informationReal nameOlivia S. BaipajGenderFemaleBirthplaceLos Angeles, CaliforniaMilitary informationService branchUS NavyGrade / RankLieutenant Junior Grade (O-2)SpecialtyCyber Warfare EngineerTrainingMassachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT),Navy Reserve Officers' Training CorpsWeapon / Vehicle expertiseM16A2 5.56mm rifle, M14 EBR 7.62 assault rifle, M11 9mm pistolOrganizational informationFaction(s)G.I. JoeMedia informationFirst comic appearanceG.I. Joe: A Real American Hero #283Black Hatis a character in theG.I. Joe Real American Herocomics fromIDWandSkybound. Black Hat is a member of G.I. Joe and a computer hacking specialist. Black Hat grew up in Pacific Palisades, and was a computer nerd from one. Her father was a digital artist who worked in TV post-production, and her mother was an IT specialist at a major software firm, so computers were always part of her life. She was the youngest person to receive a Master of Computer Science degree from MIT. She got her U.S. Navy commission through the ROTC program, and went directly into the Cyber Warfare Engineering program. Jump qualified. Qualified expert with M16A2 5.56mm rifle, M14 EBR 7.62 assault rifle, and M11 9mm pistol. Fluent in Russian, Mandarin, Farsi and Klingon.[1]

characters.name: Blue Ninja (RAH)
characters.description: TheBlue Ninjaare renegadeArashikage Clanmembers who are obsessed with technological improvements in theReal American Hero comicscontinuity. Blue NinjaMilitary informationSpecialtyMartial arts; Assassination; Covert operationsTrainingArashikage, technological enhancementsOrganizational informationFaction(s)Arashikage ClanRevancheCobraKnown mastersAlpha-001Media informationFirst comic appearanceG.I. Joe: A Real American Hero #171 The Blue Ninjas first appeared in theIDWRAH[1]comics and continued into theSkyboundRAHcomics. They are a renegade offshoot of theRussian Ninjas,who are themselves a sub-set of theArashikage Clan[1]. They operate behind the companyRevanche Robotics[2]; which is also how they upgrade themselves with cybernetic parts.

characters.name: Colonel Steve Austin
characters.description: Colonel Steve Austinis the primary protagonist of Martin Caidin's Cyborg series of novels and the television series spinoff of these books, The Six Million Dollar Man. After a almost-deadly military plane crash, as the result of a classified government project, Steve Austin was reconstructed into the world's first bionic man. Steve Austin is a secret agent who works forOscar Goldmanat OSI, the Office of Scientific Intelligence, where his speed, strength and other Bionic attributes, help him achieve the goals on his various missions. Steve Austin appears in theG.I. Joe vs. The Six Million Dollar Mancomics fromIDW/Dynamite.

characters.name: Doc (IDW)
characters.description: Docis a character in theIDWcomics related to the First Strike event. The original Doc's daughter. G.I. Joe's medic in the "Homefront" comic series. When Doc's hand was injured in battle, she guidedQuick Kickthrough the process of removing shrapnel from Ship-Wreck's chest to save him. Doc and Quick Kick appear inOptimus Primestarting in issue 10, which leads intoFirst Strike.

characters.name: Emir of Benzheen
characters.description: Emir of Benzheenis a character in theG.I. Joe RAHcomics fromMarvelandIDW. The Emir of Benzheen is an oil baron and ruthless dictator who the US has an uneasy alliance with because of oil. Several Joes were killed trying to protect the Emirate of Benzheen, for the Emir, from a Cobra invasion.--

characters.name: Faceless
characters.description: Facelessis a character who appears in theG.I. Joe Roleplaying Gameby Renegade Game Studios. He is used as a part of the introductory adventure,Snake Pit. Faceless is aCobra Officer. FormerlyCobra Commander'spersonal courier, he fell out of his employer's good graces and fled the organization. However, as he fled, he pocketed a package intended for the Commander that just happened to include the location ofThe Pit. As a way to get back into Cobra Commander's favor, he has assembled a small team known asScar Squad. His plan is to infiltrate the Pit, sabotage it and steal the G.I. Joe team's confidential data.

characters.name: Grease-Wheel
characters.description: Grease-Wheel as he appears in theG.I. Joe Roleplaying Game Grease-Wheelis a character who appears in theG.I. Joe Roleplaying Gameby Renegade Game Studios. He is used as a part of the introductory adventure,Snake Pit. QR is aMotor-Viper. He is a member ofScar Squad, who serves as the team's getaway driver. He spends his time whining and complaining, butFacelessseems to be willing to give him whatever he wants. During the course of the adventure, he steals a G.I. JoeA.P.C.so that the team can escape.

characters.name: Kirigun
characters.description: Kirigunis the main antagonist in theIDWSnake Eyes - Deadgamecomic series created byRob Liefeld. The 5 issue Deadgame IDW comics series by Rob Liefeld is story that pits Snake Eyes and friends against ancient gods and original superheroes. Using the Dead Sword in ancient times, The Grave Master fought Norse god Thor. In modern time ninjas resurrected Kirigun as a red mummy.

In the cartoon she was the daughter of Old ManFlint. In theOptimus Primecomic series, Marissa Faireborn is the Ex-Head of the Earth Defense Command.

characters.name: Middle Master
characters.description: Middle MasterPersonal informationReal namenee ArashikageGenderMaleBirthplaceJapanMilitary informationTrainingArashikage Martial ArtsOrganizational informationFaction(s)Arashikage Storm Shadow'suncle and founder of theRussian Ninjas. The younger brother of theHard Master, older brother of theSoft Master, andStorm Shadow's uncle, not much else is known about thisArashikagebrother. At some point he found himself incarcerated in a Russian Gulag. There he began training his fellow prisoners to keep morale high. One of those prisoners wasSensei Boris' father. Thus the Russian Ninja school was founded.[1]

characters.name: Milo (RAH)
characters.description: Milois a character in theReal American Herocomics fromIDW. Milo is a very skilled hacker kid.Bombstrikefinds Milo outside theRevanchefacility that her squad was infiltrating. (RAH #245) Milo helps Bombstrike get in through a sewer grate. Milo ends up joining her team, along withSightline,AmbushandBig Ben, and helping with the Revanche situation. This is during The Cobra's Venom and Artificial Intelligence story lines.

characters.name: Oscar Goldman
characters.description: Oscar Goldmanis the Director of Operations at the OSI. The character was created by Martin Caidin and introduced in the first Cyborg novel (in which he is described as director of the OSO). When the novel was adapted as the first pilot telefilm the character was replaced by another, Oliver Spencer (see "Deconstructed", below). Goldman made his television debut in the second pilot film, Wine, Women and War and appeared in virtually all episodes of The Six Million Dollar Man and The Bionic Woman that followed, as well as the later reunion movies. Richard Anderson is, to date, the only actor to have ever played the character on screen.

characters.name: Professor Grube (RAH)
characters.description: Professor Grubeis a character in the G.I. Joe A Real American Herocoloring booksfrom Golden (Western Publishing). Professor Grube is a scientist working to develop a propulsion system for the Crusader so the Joes can build a Moon Base. She's working out of a G.I. Joe base in the desert, protected by Rock 'n Roll and his team.

characters.name: QR
characters.description: QR as she appears in theG.I. Joe Roleplaying Game QRis a character who appears in theG.I. Joe Roleplaying Gameby Renegade Game Studios. She is used as a part of the introductory adventure,Snake Pit. QR is aTele-Viper. She is a member ofScar Squadand the only card-carrying member of Cobra in the team. Her loyalty was bought byFaceless, but she is willing to turn on the rest of the team in order to keep herself safe.
